item.title: Sweden Holds Rate at 1.75% and Signals Higher Chance of Hike
item.description: Sweden’s central bank held borrowing costs steady for a sixth straight meeting while indicating it’s ready to tighten policy this year if the Iran war triggers a rise in inflation.

item.title: Asia Reports Sharp Rise in Cybercrimes and Scams, Interpol Says
item.description: Illegal cyber activities accounted for around a third of all crimes recorded in some Asian countries, with scams the most widespread and financially damaging, according to a new Interpol report.

item.title: SFC Moving Ahead With HK$1 Billion Evergrande Payout Plan
item.description: Hong Kong’s market regulator is proceeding with its plan to compensate minority shareholders of collapsed developer China Evergrande Group, brushing aside a legal challenge by the company’s liquidators.

item.title: Iran to Gain Major Financial Relief Under Interim Deal With US
item.description: Iran would be allowed to start oil exports immediately under an interim deal with the US and gain access to a $300 billion economic development program following negotiations for a permanent peace that’s meant to address Tehran’s nuclear activities, according a draft of their agreement.

item.title: Japan Tourism Extends Decline on Reduced Flights, China Weakness
item.description: Japan’s inbound tourism extended its decline in May, weighed down by reduced flights across routes and a prolonged slump in Chinese visitors.

item.title: DP World Seeks Return to US Container Operations at Texas Port
item.description: DP World is looking to operate a US container port for the first time in two decades.

item.title: Lone Star Said to Lead Bidding for Continental’s Industrial Unit
item.description: Buyout firm Lone Star Funds has emerged as the frontrunner to acquire Continental AG’s industrial unit, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Green Economy Tops $10 Trillion as Revenue Growth Accelerates
item.description: The green economy — the business lines of global listed companies that generate revenue from climate solutions — now boasts a record high market value of $10 trillion.

item.title: Singapore’s Electronics Exports Jump by Record on AI Demand Boom
item.description: Singapore’s electronic exports last month rose by the most on record on robust global demand for artificial intelligence-related equipment.

item.title: India’s HDFC Bank Lands Tightest-Ever Spread on Its Dollar Bond
item.description: HDFC Bank Ltd. secured its tightest-ever spread on a dollar bond issue, showing strong investor demand for India’s largest private lender despite recent governance concerns.

item.title: UK Accused of Failing to Block Illegal Casinos After Crackdown
item.description: Britain has been urged to bolster its efforts to clamp down on illegal gambling sites after a report found users were able to access unlicensed online casinos.

item.title: UK Inflation Unexpectedly Holds Steady Ahead of BOE Decision
item.description: UK inflation unexpectedly held steady in May, suggesting price pressures were weaker than feared even before a US deal to end the Iran war sent energy costs tumbling.

item.title: BRICS Bank Lends South Africa $1 Billion for Cities, Report Says
item.description: New Development Bank is lending South Africa $1 billion for the state to upgrade infrastructure in its eight biggest cities, Business Day reported.

item.title: Gilts Rise as Benign Inflation Eases Pressure on BOE
GLP-1 Craze Fuels Protein-Powder Maker Glanbia’s Share Rally
item.description: The boom in GLP-1 weight-loss drugs is boosting demand for protein supplements and driving a rally in the stock of Glanbia Plc, a maker of nutrition products popular with gym enthusiasts and athletes.

item.title: BOK Warns AI-Driven Bonus Windfalls May Stoke Broader Inflation
item.description: South Korea’s artificial intelligence-driven semiconductor boom may wind up complicating the inflation outlook, with the central bank warning that soaring bonuses at major technology firms could fuel broader wage growth and stronger consumer demand.

item.title: Oil Tankers U-Turn, Rush to Middle East Before Hormuz Reopening
item.description: Two oil tankers heading toward Africa have u-turned in the Indian Ocean this week, switching their destinations to the Middle East as shipowners race to re-position vessels ahead of the possible reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Iron Ore Sinks Back Below $100 as Abundant Supplies Hurt Outlook
item.description: Iron ore sank below $100 a ton for the first time since March on signs of abundant seaborne supplies just as demand in China faces headwinds.

item.title: SNB Reviews Franc Alert Level Before Middle East Peace Deal
item.description: The Swiss National Bank will this week reveal if it’s too soon to dial down its franc rhetoric before a prospective Middle East peace deal becomes reality.

item.title: ECB Officials Say Peace in Iran Isn’t Enough to Fix Energy Shock
item.description: European Central Bank officials are signaling that a US-Iran peace accord won’t necessarily stop them lifting interest rates further, even if it prevents a more pronounced overshoot in inflation.

item.title: The Bad Gnus on SpaceX
item.description: With a few giants destabilizing indexes, safety in numbers isn’t what it used to be.

item.title: PBOC Launches Tool to Boost Yuan Use by Other Central Banks
item.description: China announced new money-market measures to broaden the use of its currency among entities like foreign central banks and sovereign wealth funds.

item.title: China Watchdog Urges More IPOs From AI, Hong Kong Listed Firms
item.description: China’s top securities regulator pledged to welcome more domestic listings from artificial intelligence developers as well as Hong Kong traded firms to bolster onshore capital markets.

item.title: China’s Regulator to Support Launch of Active ETF Products
item.description: China’s regulator gave the greenlight for the rollout of actively managed exchange-traded funds, expanding the range of investment products available in the world’s second‑largest stock market and bringing it closer in line with more developed peers.

item.title: Qatar Moves LNG Ships Back to Mideast Ahead of Hormuz Reopening
item.description: Qatar is beginning to bring some of its liquefied natural gas tankers back to the Middle East, as the major supplier prepares to ramp-up exports once the Strait of Hormuz reopens following a US-Iran deal.

item.title: 円安･高金利は危機でなく転機､市場を羅針盤にあるべき経済の姿語ろう
item.description: 市場の変化を単なるリスクとして恐れるのではなく、日本経済の新たな成長戦略を描くための羅針盤として生かせるか。

item.title: BOJ Keeps Market Steady With Yen Volatility at Lowest Since 2021
item.description: The Bank of Japan managed to keep financial markets relatively stable following its policy decision, leaving the yen in a narrow range that’s still historically weak.

item.title: Putin Hosts First Southeast Asia Summit Since Ukraine Invasion
item.description: President Vladimir Putin will host leaders of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations, with energy security likely to top the agenda of their first summit since Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: China’s Central Bank Hints at Shift to Fed-Style Rate Setting
item.description: China’s central bank signaled a potential shift toward an overnight policy rate, a move that would bring it closer to global peers and give policymakers greater influence over short-term funding costs.

item.title: India Micro-Caps Defy Hazy Macro With Breakout Signal
item.description: The Bloomberg India Micro Cap Index has formed a technical signal watched by traders.
